subparts.text: Define Machine Learning in terms of tasks, performance measure and training experience. Define set of tasks ‘T’, Performance measure ‘P’ and Training experience ‘E’ for the following learning problem when given to some machine: A handwriting recognition learning problem 2.5

Consider a scenario where 8,000 patients undergo flu testing. Among them, 7,000 are healthy and 1,000 are sick. The test results show that out of the sick patients, 600 tested positive and 400 tested negative. Among the healthy patients, 200 tested positive and 6,800 tested negative. Construct a confusion matrix based on this data and calculate the precision and recall. 2.5
What is a Perceptron? Explain the working of a perceptron with the help of a neat diagram. 2.5
Define entropy. Given the probabilities of five events where P1 = 0.5, and P2 = P3= P4= P5= 0.125 , determine the entropy of the system 2.5
Explain Bayesian Network with the help of an example. 2.5
What is a Bag of Words approach for document classification in machine learning? State its two disadvantages. 2.5
State two advantages of hierarchical clustering over K-means clustering. Explain single linkage and complete linkage functions used in hierarchical clustering. 2.5
State difference between SARSA and Q-Learning approaches. 2.5

questions.question_text: Consider a dataset having dependent variable Y and independent variable X as given by Y = T and independent variable X= T having a linear relationship. Execute an iteration of the Gradient Descent algorithm to fine tune slope and intercept coefficient . Consider initial values for w1 = 2, w0 = 1 and learning rate = 0.3. Write the updated values of w0 and w1. Find initial mean squared error and new mean squared error.

Using least squares method, find the best fit linear regression coefficients for the data given below. Also predict the value of Y for X =15 using learned coefficients. Find the mean squared error for the given dataset using your learned hypothesis. X Y 2 21 4 27 6 29 8 64 10 86

Consider the following neural network with initial weights, biases and input/outputs as mentioned. Given the inputs x1 = 0.9 and x2 = 0.3, determine the outputs predicted at the output units o1 and o2. Calculate the prediction error Etotal. Show the outputs of hidden units as well. Use sigmoid function as the activation function for hidden as well the output layers.

Consider following training data for 5 persons. For binary classification of a person as sick or not sick create a decision tree model. Show all the steps. Person No A1 A2 A3 Class 1 Yes Yes Yes Not Sick 2 Yes No Yes Sick 3 No No Yes Sick 4 No Yes Yes Not Sick 5 No Yes No Sick

Apply Naïve Bayesian Classifier to Predict whether a car is stolen or not with features {Color:RED, Origin:Domestic, Type:SUV} based on given dataset. Color Type Origin Stolen RED SPORTS DOMESTIC YES RED SPORTS DOMESTIC NO RED SPORTS DOMESTIC YES YELLOW SPORTS DOMESTIC NO YELLOW SPORTS IMPORTED YES YELLOW SUV IMPORTED NO YELLOW SUV IMPORTED YES YELLOW SUV DOMESTIC NO RED SUV IMPORTED NO RED SPORTS IMPORTED YES

Consider the dataset given below to classify a new instance with A1=2 and A2=5 as positive or negative using K-Nearest Neighbor method. Use value for ‘K’ as 5 and distance function as Euclidean distance. A1 A2 Y 2 3 - 5 7 + 6 6 + 1 2 - 7 5 + 1 4 - 4 1 - 7 3 +

State difference between supervised and unsupervised machine learning. Cluster the following set of data objects in two clusters by applying one iteration of k-means algorithm. Treat objects 2 and 5 as initial cluster centers. Use Euclidean distance as the distance metric. Determine updated cluster center coordinates. Object Number X-coordinate Y-coordinate 1 2 4 2 4 6 3 6 8 4 10 4 5 12 4
Explain following :  Markov Decision process  Positive and negative reinforcement learning
